rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Bayer Cropscience Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS CASTAGNA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Nibco Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages) because those claims have been dismissed. Plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he ws exposed to original packing in Nibco¿s valves. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY LEE FULLER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY LEE FULLER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Nibco Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is off calendar because plaintiff has dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages. Due to plaintiff¿s deposition testimony, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess evidence that he was exposed to original packing in Nibco valves. Liberally construed, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony is sufficient to preclude defendant¿s reliance on the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers was not considered because plaintiff was not provided an opportunity to respond to that evidence. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL LEAL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC, SUCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ABEX Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing friction products attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay and plaintiff¿s citation to his own interrogatory responses to create a triable issue are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE VALLADON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors¿ joinder to defendant Washington Group International Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs failed to serve defendant with the amended complaint within two years of filing of the wrongful death action. =(220/HEK)
